marketState: neutral with bearish lean; above-average demand fighting above-average storage and bearish technicals
summary: Front-month NG=F sits at $3.156, modestly above the fair-value midpoint of $2.958 but well within the $2.458–$3.458 band, so the market is not dangerously stretched in either direction. The single clearest bullish signal is current weather demand running +17% above the 5-year normal at 148.4 and spiking +7.6 in 24 hours, which supports near-term power-burn and creates short-squeeze risk given that managed-money net positioning is at the 17th percentile — extremely short by recent history. Countering this, storage sits +153 Bcf above the 5-year average, a structural ceiling on sustained rallies, and technicals are broadly bearish across the 5m through daily and weekly timeframes. Seasonality offers a mild near-term edge (56–69% probability higher over weeks 1–3) that reverses sharply by week 4 (31% probability, typical move –2.3%) with very low reliability (44pp 10–90th percentile spread). Critical data gaps — granular 1–7D and 8–16D weather demand metrics, forward fair-value projections (plus7D/14D/21D), and the futures curve — all returned null, meaningfully reducing conviction on any multi-week directional call.

longTerm.rationale: The 4-week seasonal probability is just 31% higher with a typical –2.3% expected move, and storage at +153 Bcf above the 5-year average suppresses the case for a sustained rally. Price is already slightly above the fair-value midpoint ($3.156 vs $2.958) and forward fair-value data is unavailable, leaving the band trajectory unknown. The extreme COT short (17th percentile) is intriguing as a contrarian signal, but September is the seasonally strongest month and a better entry near fair value ($2.95–3.05) would offer substantially improved risk/reward for capturing that window.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage builds stop shrinking vs. the 5-year average; price fails to recover fair value on any pullback; demand trend reverses below 5-year normal.
longTerm.trigger: Enter LONG when price pulls back to the $2.95–3.05 fair-value zone AND/OR EIA weekly storage reports begin printing consistent below-forecast surprises (surplus vs. 5y narrows to under 80 Bcf), signaling hot-weather demand is meaningfully eroding the structural overhang.

shortTerm.stop: $2.95 (below fair-value midpoint; a close here negates the bounce thesis and risks band re-test at $2.46)
shortTerm.sizing: 0.5R approx 0.5% account risk — half-sized given missing weather detail data and bearish technical structure
shortTerm.rationale: Current gas demand at 148.4 (+17% vs 5-year normal, +7.6 intraday) is the dominant near-term driver; sustained heat keeps power-burn elevated and tightens the daily balance. The 1-week and 2-week seasonal probabilities favor the upside (56% each) and the 3-week is 69%, providing a statistical tailwind. With managed money at the 17th percentile (5-year), any bullish headline could trigger forced short-covering; 1-hour RSI at 35.5 supports at least a tactical bounce from current levels.
shortTerm.invalidation: Price closes below $2.95, confirming the bearish EMA structure (50 < 200 on 5m/15m/1d/1w) is dominant and the weather-demand pop was insufficient to reverse the trend.

keyDrivers: Weather demand running +17% above 5-year normal (148.4 units) and rising sharply (+7.6 in 24h) — primary near-term bullish support
Managed-money net at 17th percentile (–84,909 contracts) — extreme short positioning vulnerable to squeeze on any bullish catalyst
Storage +153 Bcf above 5-year average — structural bearish ceiling on sustained multi-week rallies
Seasonal probability positive for weeks 1–3 (56/56/69%) but turns bearish at week 4 (31%); typical 4-week move is –2.3%
Technicals broadly bearish (5m/15m/1d/1w trend bearish, EMA 50 < 200); 1h/4h neutral with RSI approaching oversold at 1h (35.5)
risks: Critical data gaps: 1–7D and 8–16D weather demand metrics are null, forward fair-value projections are null, and the futures curve is unavailable — confidence in any directional call is materially reduced
Storage surplus (+153 Bcf vs 5y) could persist or widen if demand normalizes after the heat event, reasserting downward pressure toward fair value and potentially the band floor near $2.46
Technical breakdown below $3.00 (and especially a daily close below fair-value mid of $2.958) would confirm the bearish EMA structure across timeframes and open a move toward the $2.46 band low
